steps.title: Read off x directly
steps.content: The first equation is $x = 5$. This is already solved — no algebra needed. The solution must have $x = 5$.

steps.title: Substitute into the second equation
steps.content:
The second equation is $y = x - 8$. Plug in $x = 5$:
$$y = 5 - 8 = -3$$
The solution is $(5, -3)$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$(0, 0)$': $x = 0 e 5$, so this fails the first equation immediately. ✗
  • Choice B — '$(5, -3)$': $x = 5$ ✓ and $y = 5 - 8 = -3$ ✓. Both equations satisfied. ✓
  • Choice C — '$(5, -8)$': $x = 5$ ✓, but $y = 5 - 8 = -3 e -8$. Fails the second equation. ✗
  • Choice D — '$(5, 8)$': $x = 5$ ✓, but $y = 5 - 8 = -3 e 8$. The sign is wrong. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
